title: Bi-temporal valid-time windows for decision graph time-travel queries
context: Hunch needs to support querying what was believed about code at any past point (for regression analysis, understanding decision evolution, debugging). Mutable records lose this history. Git commit timestamps are authoritative—anchor temporal queries there. Touches invariant(s): con_2ce3f2a547 (Synthesis must run on the Claude subscription, never the pay-per-token API: strip ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from the spawned env); con_902759b3dc (All JSON writes to .hunch/ must be atomic (temp-file + rename) so an interrupted write can never truncate the index); con_947c578b2c (Forward-migrate raw JSON to the current schema BEFORE Zod validation; never silently drop unmigratable records); con_a87360128b (Semantic vectors are a derived layer in SQLite, never the source of truth; reconcile by content hash on reindex); con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Implement bitemporal schema: add valid_from/valid_to/superseded_by/retired fields to decisions and constraints. All queries (why, context, etc.) get optional --as-of <git-ref> to filter records through valid-time windows. Supersede instead of delete to preserve history immutably. Backfill via lossless v2 migration using each record's date.
consequences: Can query decision graph at any historical commit, enabling regression analysis and decision archaeology
Records are append-only; full audit trail built-in (no deletion means intent is preserved)
Foundation laid for Stage 2 enforcement (regression guard can now check if a change violates constraints that were active when the change was made)
All query paths (store.why, store.checkConstraints, store.assembleContext) now filter by asOf window—more complex but enables temporal correctness
v2 migration with backfill required; no data is dropped but schema complexity increases by 4 fields per record
alternatives_rejected: Simple mutation/deletion: loses all history, impossible to answer 'what did we believe then?' or support regression checking
Transaction-time only: records when Hunch learned something, not when developers intended it—git author-date is the semantic anchor
Manual external versioning (branches/copies): git timestamps are already authoritative and automatic; would require redundant bookkeeping
Snapshot-based (frozen copies at each commit): valid-time windows are more compact and easier to query than separate snapshots
